questions.question: What is a Soil Health Card?
questions.answer: Soil Health Card is a printed report given to farmers containing status of soil fertility and other associated parameters that determine crop productivity.

questions.question: How to interpret NPK values in Soil Health Card?
questions.answer: NPK stands for Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), and Potassium (K). Low values indicate deficiency requiring fertilizer application. Optimal ranges: N (200-250 kg/ha), P (20-30 kg/ha), K (40-60 kg/ha) for most crops.

questions.question: What does pH value indicate?
questions.answer: pH indicates soil acidity or alkalinity. Ideal pH for most crops is 6.5-7.5. Below 6.5 is acidic (add lime), above 7.5 is alkaline (add gypsum or organic matter).

questions.question: What is MSP for wheat in 2024?
questions.answer: Minimum Support Price for wheat for 2024-25 is ₹2,275 per quintal.

questions.question: What is MSP for paddy/rice?
questions.answer: Minimum Support Price for paddy (common variety) is ₹2,183 per quintal and for Grade A paddy is ₹2,203 per quintal for 2024-25.

questions.question: How to sell on e-NAM?
questions.answer: Register at enam.gov.in, link your APMC mandi account, get quality assaying done, and participate in online bidding. Payment is directly transferred to your bank account.

questions.question: What is PMFBY?
questions.answer: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana is a crop insurance scheme providing financial support to farmers suffering crop loss/damage due to unforeseen events like natural calamities, pests & diseases.

questions.question: What is the premium rate under PMFBY?
questions.answer: Farmers pay: 2% premium for Kharif food & oilseed crops, 1.5% for Rabi food & oilseed crops, and 5% for commercial/horticultural crops. Government subsidizes the rest.

questions.question: How to file a claim under PMFBY?
questions.answer: Report crop damage to local agriculture department/patwari within 72 hours of occurrence. Provide land records, Aadhaar, and bank details. Claim amount is settled after crop cutting experiments.

questions.question: How to control wheat rust?
questions.answer: For leaf/stripe rust: Spray Propiconazole 25% EC @ 0.1% or Tebuconazole 25.9% EC @ 0.1%. Remove and burn infected plant debris. Use resistant varieties like HD-3086, HD-3226.

questions.question: How to control pest in cotton?
questions.answer: For bollworm: Install pheromone traps (4-5/acre), spray Bt formulation or Emamectin Benzoate 5% SG @ 0.4g/liter. For aphids: Spray Imidacloprid 17.8% SL @ 0.3ml/liter.

questions.question: How to manage blight in potato?
questions.answer: For late blight: Spray Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2.5g/liter or Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP @ 2.5g/liter. Ensure proper drainage and avoid overhead irrigation.
